title: Guapira bracei
intro: Guapira bracei, also known as the "Wild Madder", is a woody vine native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Guapira bracei include iridoids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Guapira bracei is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and may also be used to make teas, tinctures, and capsules, allowing for a range of traditional herbal preparations to be made with this plant.
taxonomy: Guapira bracei is a species of flowering plant in the family Nyctaginaceae. It belongs to the genus Guapira, which is native to the Americas. The plant is also known as a member of the four o'clock family, Nyctaginaceae. Guapira bracei is further classified under the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta. This classification helps to categorize and understand the plant's evolutionary relationships and characteristics.
